questions.text: What is one value or principle you hold very dear — where do you think it came from?
What does 'success' mean to you personally — not what the world says, but what feels true for you?
What is one thing you will never compromise on, no matter the situation?
How do you decide what is right or wrong when there's no clear rule?
What role does honesty play in your life — how far would you go to protect it?
How do you show respect to other people, even when you disagree with them?
What principle or belief guides your biggest life decisions the most?
Was there a moment when you had to choose between two values that were both important to you?
What do you believe is the most important way to treat other people?
Is there a value you used to hold strongly that you no longer believe — what changed your mind?
Looking back, which of your core values has been the hardest to live by — and why?
What one guiding principle would you want written on your tombstone or remembered by?
What do you hope your children, grandchildren or loved ones carry forward from your values?
If you could pass on only one core belief or lesson to the next generation, what would it be?
Is there a value you wish society cared more about — and how do you try to live it anyway?
What principle do you want to be remembered for embodying, even on your hardest days?
